title: componentWillMount is deprecated
message: componentWillMount was deprecated in React 16.3.0 and removed in React 18. It was commonly used for side effects that should not happen before the component is mounted.
suggestion: Use `constructor()` for initialization logic or `componentDidMount()` for side effects like API calls.

title: componentWillReceiveProps is deprecated
message: componentWillReceiveProps was deprecated in React 16.3.0 and removed in React 18. It was error-prone and often used incorrectly.
suggestion: Use `getDerivedStateFromProps()` for deriving state from props, or use `componentDidUpdate()` for side effects on prop changes (with a comparison check).

title: componentWillUpdate is deprecated
message: componentWillUpdate was deprecated in React 16.3.0 and removed in React 18. It ran before rendering and could not safely access the DOM or perform side effects.
suggestion: Use `getSnapshotBeforeUpdate()` for reading DOM state before a render, and `componentDidUpdate()` for side effects after a render.

title: ReactDOM.render() is deprecated in React 18
message: ReactDOM.render() was deprecated in React 18 in favor of the new concurrent renderer. It still works but logs a warning and will be removed in a future version.

title: findDOMNode() is deprecated
message: findDOMNode() was deprecated in React 16.6.0 and removed in React 18. It breaks component abstraction and cannot be used with function components.
suggestion: Pass a ref directly to the DOM element you need to access. Use `React.createRef()` or the `useRef()` hook in function components.

title: String refs are deprecated
message: String refs (e.g., ref="myRef") were deprecated in React 16 and removed in React 18. They have several issues including performance and composition problems.
suggestion: Use `React.createRef()` in class components or `useRef()` in function components: `ref={this.myRef}` or `ref={myRef}`.

title: Legacy Context API (childContextTypes) is deprecated
message: The legacy Context API using childContextTypes, getChildContext(), and contextTypes was deprecated in React 16.3.0 and removed in React 18. It has performance issues and cannot be updated properly with shouldComponentUpdate.
suggestion: Use the modern Context API with `React.createContext()`, `Context.Provider`, and `useContext()` hook or `Context.Consumer`.

title: Legacy Context API (getChildContext) is deprecated
message: getChildContext() was deprecated in React 16.3.0 alongside childContextTypes. It is part of the legacy Context API that was removed in React 18.
suggestion: Use `React.createContext()` with a Provider component to pass data down the component tree.

title: Legacy Context API (contextTypes) is deprecated
message: The contextTypes static property was deprecated alongside childContextTypes in React 16.3.0 and removed in React 18. It was used to consume legacy context.
suggestion: Use `React.useContext()` hook, or `Context.Consumer` render prop with the modern Context API.

title: createReactClass() is deprecated
message: createReactClass() was part of the deprecated react-create-class package. It was a legacy way to create React components before ES2015 classes were adopted.
suggestion: Use ES2015 class components: `class MyComponent extends React.Component { render() { ... } }`, or use function components with hooks.

title: PropTypes from 'react' is deprecated
message: Importing PropTypes from the 'react' package was deprecated in React 15.5. PropTypes were removed from the React core to reduce the bundle size.
suggestion: Install the `prop-types` package separately and import PropTypes from 'prop-types'. Or use TypeScript for static type checking instead of runtime PropTypes.

title: React.createFactory() is deprecated
message: React.createFactory() was deprecated in React 16 and removed in React 18. It returned a factory function for creating React elements of a given type.

title: React.createClass is deprecated
message: React.createClass() was deprecated in React 15.5 and removed from the React core package. It was the original way to create components before ES2015 classes.
suggestion: Use ES2015 class components with `extends React.Component` or function components with hooks.

title: componentDidMount setState should be used sparingly
message: Calling setState synchronously in componentDidMount is not deprecated, but AI-generated code often uses it to fetch data when it should use a proper data-fetching strategy. Multiple setState calls in componentDidMount cause extra re-renders.
suggestion: Use useEffect with async functions in function components, or batch setState calls in componentDidMount. Consider using React Query, SWR, or a similar data-fetching library.

title: UNSAFE_componentWillMount should be migrated away
message: The UNSAFE_ prefix lifecycle methods (UNSAFE_componentWillMount, UNSAFE_componentWillReceiveProps, UNSAFE_componentWillUpdate) are available as migration aids but are deprecated and will be removed in future React versions.
suggestion: Migrate to the recommended alternatives: componentDidMount for side effects, getDerivedStateFromProps for state derivation, and getSnapshotBeforeUpdate for DOM reads.

title: UNSAFE_componentWillReceiveProps should be migrated away
message: UNSAFE_componentWillReceiveProps is a migration alias for the deprecated componentWillReceiveProps. It will be removed in a future React version.
suggestion: Use getDerivedStateFromProps for state derivation from props, or componentDidUpdate with a comparison check for side effects.

title: UNSAFE_componentWillUpdate should be migrated away
message: UNSAFE_componentWillUpdate is a migration alias for the deprecated componentWillUpdate. It will be removed in a future React version.
suggestion: Use getSnapshotBeforeUpdate for reading DOM state, and componentDidUpdate for side effects after rendering.
